According to the attached document from 2026-06-28, Turn 161 is again an attachment-only continuation. The three required current-turn files are present, and I used only those current attachments: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md. I did not substitute any File Library material.

Turn 161 ‚ÄĒ attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 160:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(őďS‚ČÖGőďH,¬†NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The original target is exactly the over-G isomorphism question: whether there exists a continuous isomorphismő¶:őďS‚ąľőďH such thatŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.The problem statement explicitly defines this as the desired isomorphism over the fixed tame quotient G, with both groups carrying the canonical maps to G.The attached prompt asks for exactly this proof-or-disproof task.The theorem coordinate remains closed. The attached history records the stronger F=P‚čäG-level statement:‚ąÉ¬†ő¶:F‚ąľ

F¬†over¬†Gsuch¬†thatő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH.

Since rS and rS‚ąí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this gives ő¶(‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,and hence descends to

This is exactly the requested condition ŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.No structural computation should be reopened under the present inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen¬†equivalence+residual¬†restricted-Lie¬†induction+normalized¬†Koszul‚ÄďWitt¬†coefficient¬†induction+Certificates¬†D.1‚ÄďD.4+finite¬†filtered¬†Nielsen¬†compactness‚üĻőďS‚ČÖGőďH.The uniform reason is unchanged: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization of the local row to (2,t) and the Koszul‚ÄďWitt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite filtered solutions.The compactness coordinate also remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one. For coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms. Each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer and gives a finite over-G automorphism; a compatible inverse limit gives ő¶:F‚ÜíF with ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains unchanged. The current attachments are still only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1‚ÄďD.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule applies again:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}‚üĻ(őďS‚ČÖGőďH,¬†NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 161 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 160. The theorem coordinate remains őďS‚ČÖGőďH, via an over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF satisfying ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1‚ÄďD.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1‚ÄďD.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN161-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
